key_insight: The paper uses thermo-mechanical simulation to identify and compare the conditions that trigger flash formation during inertia friction welding of FGH98 powder superalloy.
engineering_relevance: Its results can guide process-window selection and parameter control to reduce flash defects and improve weld quality in high-performance superalloy joining.
method: Built a coupled thermo-mechanical simulation of inertia friction welding.
Performed comparative screening of flash-formation behavior under different process conditions.
Analyzed weld response for FGH98 powder superalloy to isolate defect-promoting regimes.
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a simulation-based comparative screening study published as a journal article, but provides no quantitative results in the summary.
watch_item: Because the evidence described in the abstract is simulation-centered, experimental validation would be important before direct shop-floor adoption.
paper_title: Thermo-mechanical simulation and comparative screening of flash formation in inertia friction welding of FGH98 powder superalloy
